title: Number Systems, Signed Quantities, and Reliable Operations · Assumption Clinic
summary: Reconcile inventory gains, losses, temperatures, elevations, and account changes from mixed records. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.
Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.
Model direction and change with signed numbers and explain each operation on a number line.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Rounding Precision for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation.
steps.why: The Rounding Precision result for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Round only at a justified reporting boundary while preserving sufficient working precision.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Rounding Precision, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Round only at a justified reporting boundary while preserving sufficient working precision.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Round only at a justified reporting boundary while preserving sufficient working precision.
